headline: RBI provision of ₹1,283 Cr for agri-PSL non-compliance impacts FY26 earnings; urgent board action required.
board_paragraph: ICICI Bank must provision ₹1,283 Cr (from article) following RBI’s directive for non-compliance with agricultural PSL norms, directly impacting Q4 FY26 earnings. The board should approve a comprehensive PSL compliance remediation plan within 8 weeks to avoid further regulatory penalties and governance downgrades. Failure to act risks additional enforcement by RBI and SEBI, potential rating downgrades, and up to ₹1,283 Cr (from article) in further direct costs, alongside reputational and investor trust erosion. Immediate action is critical to contain financial and ESG fallout.

stakeholder_map.stance: Enforcement escalation if remediation plan absent within 30-60 days. Formal show-cause → interim penalty → appeal window. Settlement via Consent Mechanism in 40-60% of cases.
Direct intervention via moratorium, AQR, or governance restructuring for material asset-quality or fraud events. Climate stress test framework applies to top-16 banks from FY27.

AGAINST vote recommendation on audit committee chair or CEO re-election for material governance failures. Downgrade of governance rating (MSCI/ISS QualityScore) by 1-2 bands. AGM season cadence: April-September.
stakeholder_map.precedent: Infosys 2017 Sikka exit → ISS flagged 2 AGMs

stakeholder_map.stance: Rating action (e.g., BBB → BB) within 90-180 days of material event. Watch-list status in interim. Flow-through to MSCI ESG Leaders / SRI indices triggers 1 (engine estimate)-3% AUM outflow from passive strategies.
stakeholder_map.precedent: Vedanta 2020 B→CCC → MSCI ESG Leaders exclusion

stakeholder_map.stakeholder: BlackRock + Norges Bank IM + CalPERS (Top-10 AUM)
stakeholder_map.stance: Private engagement first (letter/meeting) within 30-60 days. Vote against if remediation absent by next AGM. Exclusion from ESG mandate AUM if severity=high sustained 2+ quarters. Position review on 8-12% AUM flows.
stakeholder_map.precedent: NBIM 2017 excluded 11 coal miners incl. Adani Ports

three_year_trajectory.do_nothing: If no remediation is undertaken, by FY27-29, ICICI Bank faces sustained earnings drag from the ₹1,283 Cr (from article) provision, likely further regulatory penalties, and a probable downgrade in credit and ESG ratings. Investor confidence may erode, triggering 1-3% AUM outflows and a 20-40 bps (engine estimate) increase in cost of capital. Proxy advisors may recommend against key board reappointments, and workforce attrition could rise, compounding operational risk.
three_year_trajectory.act_now: Immediate board approval of a PSL compliance remediation plan, at an estimated implementation cost of ₹80-100 Cr (engine estimate), can contain the impact to the mandated ₹1,283 Cr (from article) provision. This action is expected to restore ESG ratings, stabilize investor sentiment, and reduce cost of capital by 20-40 bps (engine estimate) within 12-18 months, positioning the bank for recovery by FY28.

qna_drafts.earnings_call: The RBI-mandated ₹1,283 Cr (from article) provision for agri-PSL non-compliance will impact our Q4 FY26 results. We are implementing a robust remediation plan to ensure full PSL compliance and restore stakeholder confidence. Our focus remains on governance, transparency, and long-term value creation.
qna_drafts.press_statement: ICICI Bank acknowledges the RBI’s directive to provision ₹1,283 Cr (from article) for agricultural PSL non-compliance. We are taking immediate steps to strengthen our compliance systems and are committed to full remediation within the stipulated timeline. Our goal is to restore trust and uphold the highest governance standards.
qna_drafts.board_qa: Q: What is the maximum penalty exposure? A: The direct provision required is ₹1,283 Cr (from article); further penalties could arise if remediation is delayed, but timely action should contain exposure. Q: What is the FY26 capex impact? A: The provision impacts earnings, not capex; remediation system upgrades are estimated at ₹80-100 Cr (engine estimate), spread over FY26-FY27. Q: Will this affect board reappointments? A: Proxy advisors may recommend against re-election if remediation is not demonstrated before the next AGM.
qna_drafts.regulator_qa: We acknowledge the RBI’s directive and are fully committed to implementing a comprehensive PSL compliance remediation plan within 8 weeks. We will provide regular progress updates and ensure all corrective actions are completed within the stipulated timeframe. We welcome ongoing engagement with RBI and SEBI to ensure full compliance and governance alignment.

warnings: verifier: added 3 source tags on ₹ figures
